description: Trade the first 15-minute opening range breakout on NQ futures during the US Regular session. Enter on confirmed close above or below the range; exit at 1.5x range extension or time stop.
entry_rules:
1. Define the opening range: high and low of the 09:30–09:45 ET candles on the 1-min chart.
2. Enter LONG on first 1-min close above the range high. Volume must be above 20-period average.
3. Enter SHORT on first 1-min close below the range low. Same volume filter applies.
4. One direction only per session — do not reverse.
5. No new entries after 10:30 ET.
exit_rules:
Stop: opposite side of the opening range.
Target: 1.5x opening range width projected from the breakout level.
Time exit: close all positions by 11:00 ET if target not reached.
Do not hold through 10:30 ET without a profit.
risk_rules:
Max 1 trade per session.
Max daily loss: 2x opening range width in points.
Position size: 1 contract (Sim101 only until human-approved).
No re-entry after stop out.
why_edge_exists: Opening range breakouts capture directional momentum established in the first 15 minutes of the US session. Institutional order flow frequently drives follow-through on confirmed breakouts above/below the initial range.
why_strategy_may_fail: Low-volatility days and choppy open conditions produce false breakouts with immediate reversals. Gap opens wider than the range can invalidate the setup before entry is possible.
filters:
Avoid days where overnight range exceeds 2x the average opening range.
Avoid first trading day of the month (often erratic opens).
optimization_variables: Range period: 10–20 min. Target multiplier: 1.0–2.5x. Volume threshold: 1.0–1.5x average.
